source_rfp: Service Logic - Field Service Management Platform RFP (Version E, 2026-06-02)
source_instrument: Service Logic FSM RFP - Vendor Scorecard v1.xlsx
note: Two scoring lenses exist and are both encoded. (1) SSA scorecard categories (this file) and (2) the RFP Section 30 capability-weighted lens (capabilities.json). The agent reports both; the SSA category weighting drives the headline weighted total.

categories.name: Understanding of Service Logic
categories.good_looks_like: Reflects the multi-OpCo, PE-backed, acquisitive, union/CBA reality and the Work-to-Cash priority; not a generic FSM pitch.

categories.name: Completeness of Response
categories.good_looks_like: Every requirement carries a response code; all 28 qualification and all pricing questions answered with specifics; demo-ready.

categories.name: Vendor Platform Architecture
categories.good_looks_like: Single-tenant topology w/ SOC2 Type II; proven scale to 80+ entities / 2,000+ concurrent / 3,500+ techs; open API + event integration; offline mobile; upgrade-safe config; security & DR posture.

categories.name: Vendor Qualifications
categories.good_looks_like: Financial stability, HVAC/union/multi-entity references at comparable scale, credible implementation model, real customer-success structure.

categories.name: Requirement Alignment
categories.good_looks_like: OOB/CONFIG coverage of Must and Should requirements across all functional domains, with evidence; CUSTOM/ROADMAP minimized and justified.

categories.name: Financials & Commercial Model
categories.good_looks_like: Transparent, role-based, volume-tiered pricing; no hidden fees; free data export; competitive 3-/5-year TCO for Service Logic scale.

quality_scale.5: High - Excellent: fully meets the need out-of-the-box; differentiated, proven, well-evidenced.
quality_scale.4: Good: meets the need via standard configuration; strong, credible response.
quality_scale.3: Average/Adequate: meets the need but with caveats, moderate effort, or thin evidence.
quality_scale.2: Below Average: partially meets the need; meaningful gaps, customization, or risk.
quality_scale.1: Low - Poor: does not meaningfully meet the need; gap, vague, or unsupported.

met_values.Partial: Partially provided; some aspects unmet or need work.
met_values.No: Not provided and no acceptable path to it.

response_codes.OOB: Out of the box - fully supported in current GA release, no modifications.
response_codes.CONFIG: Configurable via admin configuration, no code changes.
response_codes.EXTENSION: Built using the product's extensible framework.
response_codes.CUSTOM: Custom development - scripting or code-level changes required.
response_codes.PARTNER: Delivered via certified third-party / ISV / marketplace partner.
response_codes.ROADMAP: Planned / not yet available; published future release.
response_codes.GAP: Not supported and no published roadmap.

moscow.Must: Non-negotiable. A 'No' (or unsupported ROADMAP/CUSTOM without firm SOW) on any Must is a basis for disqualification.
moscow.Should: High priority; expected of a market-leading platform. Absence hurts scoring.
moscow.Could: Desirable differentiator; not required for initial deployment.
moscow.Won't: Explicitly out of scope; no response required.

gating_rules.description: Any Must marked No -> disqualifying. A Must answered ROADMAP, GAP, or CUSTOM-without-SOW is treated as effectively unmet for gating. Single-tenant deployment and absolute entity-level union/non-union data isolation are hard architectural gates.

raise: Concrete evidence cited (references, screenshots, named customers)
lower: Capability must be validated in live demo (Appendix F)

confidence_model.rule: Every score carries a confidence level and an explicit list of evidence gaps that must be closed (typically in the July 13-16 Charlotte demos or reference checks).
